Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step.reason: The paper presents PCL as the missing side-chain activation step needed to obtain 17 in their system.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: PCL is directly linked to production of 3'-N-debenzoyl-2'-deoxypaclitaxel (17).

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: 17 is a late paclitaxel precursor, a taxane diterpenoid.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: 17 is described as a late paclitaxel precursor toward the anti-cancer drug Taxol.

End_Product_Connection_Type.reason: PCL supplies the activated side-chain precursor required for direct late product formation.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: All three points are explicitly stated in the Results/Fig. 5e,f.

Other_Important_Info.reason: The paper explicitly notes previously proposed AAEs failed in their system and AAE-867.5 resolved this.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: Candidate prioritization from expression modules, transient co-expression, feeding and de novo pathway assays, and LC-MS detection are described.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: AAE-867.5 is described as an acyl-activating enzyme (AAE).

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: The paper reports heterologous production of 17 in N. benthamiana leaves.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: The paper says examination of top AAEs in Taxol expression modules revealed the candidate AAE-867.5.

Applied_Promoters.reason: pEAQ-HT vector use is supported but specific promoter identity is not stated.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: The enzyme was transiently expressed in infiltrated leaves.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: The Results describe transient pathway reconstitution and feeding of baccatin III in N. benthamiana leaves.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Results support enabling side-chain installation to produce 17.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: PAM and BAPT are pathway partners in co-expression assays, but no direct protein interaction data are provided.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: The paper directly shows it extends baccatin III engineering to a late paclitaxel precursor.

Omics_Data.reason: mpXsn/module evidence and LC-MS are supported, but GEO accession GSE292840 is not present in the provided paper text.

corrections.reason: PAM and BAPT are pathway partners in co-expression assays, but no direct protein interaction data are provided.

corrections.old_value: snRNA-seq/mpXsn, GEO: GSE292840; metabolomics by LC-MS
